top3: SMC Combined: the 'non-repainting' claim in the top comment is CONDITIONAL — signals actively repaint when 'Only signal on closed bars' is turned OFF. The comment gives no warning about this.

SMC Lite: first-bar false alert — on load, a spurious tap alert can fire if price already sits inside a watched zone, because bar[−1] is na (Pine treats it as 'not inside'), creating a false entry edge on bar 0.

SMC Combined: HTF is set to 4× the chart timeframe, producing non-standard levels like 4-minute or 20-minute. Snapping to 5m / 15m / 1h / 4h / Daily / Weekly gives cleaner, tradeable reference levels.

files.description: Confirmation-stacked BUY/SELL arrows using a pullback-to-EMA-reclaim trigger. Quality-graded supply/demand zones, fair value gaps, prior-day + prior-session levels, auto-adapt-to-timeframe, and a live bias dashboard. Signals require a configurable number of confirmations to pass: HTF trend agreement, RSI momentum turn, above-average volume, and zone/level location confluence.

badges.label: Non-Repainting
badges.note: Only true when 'Only signal on closed bars' is ON (the default). Signals actively repaint when it is OFF — arrows appear mid-bar, then vanish if price reverses before close.

badges.label: Alerts Match Signals
badges.note: alertcondition(buy,...) and alertcondition(sell,...) use the exact same buy/sell variables as plotshape(). No mismatch.

badges.label: HTF Lookahead
badges.note: HTF bias uses lookahead=barmerge.lookahead_off. Prior-day levels use lookahead_on with [1] offset — the standard non-repainting daily-level idiom. Verified correct.

badges.label: Auto-Adapt
badges.note: Works correctly across 1m–60m. One edge case on daily+ charts (minDrawGrEff=2 may show no zones). See Medium issue #2.

high.title: Non-repainting claim in the top comment is not conditional — creates false confidence
high.explanation: The top comment says 'no-repaint' as if it is always guaranteed. In reality, the 'Only signal on closed bars' input (confirmClose) is what makes signals non-repainting. When a user turns this OFF, BUY/SELL arrows can appear mid-bar and then VANISH before the candle closes — that is the textbook definition of repainting. A new trader who reads the header and trusts the claim without noticing the toggle could backtest a strategy thinking it is repaint-free, while actually testing on mid-bar phantom signals.
high.location: Top comment block, approximately line 4 — 'you can toggle on/off (no-repaint...'

medium.title: HTF = 4× chart timeframe creates non-standard levels (4m, 20m, 80m...)
medium.explanation: The higher-timeframe bias is computed as htf = timeframe.from_seconds(timeframe.in_seconds() * 4). On a 1-minute chart this gives a 4-minute HTF; on a 5-minute chart, a 20-minute HTF. TradingView constructs these on the fly from its standard bars. Most traders watch 5m, 15m, 1h, 4h, and daily — the 4x multiplier almost never lands on one of those clean levels. The EMAs used for bias are therefore computed on synthetic candles that most traders never see, and the bias can flip at different moments than the charts being watched.
medium.location: After the inputs section, approximately where htf is defined

medium.title: Auto-adapt sets minDrawGrEff=2 on daily+ charts — most users will see NO zones
medium.explanation: On daily and higher timeframes, the auto-adapt code requires a zone to score 2 or more grade points out of a possible 3 (displacement, sweep, tight width). In practice, very few daily pivots earn all three simultaneously — a strong impulsive move that also swept above a prior high AND left a tight, precise zone is genuinely rare. Most users loading this indicator on a daily chart will wonder why no supply/demand zones appear at all. Lowering the daily+ threshold to 1 still filters out completely ungraded zones while keeping useful levels visible.
medium.location: Auto-adapt section — the minDrawGrEff calculation line
medium.fixLocation: minDrawGrEff calculation — the single value at the end of the ternary chain

medium.title: Dashboard position does not update after the indicator loads — changing it has no effect
medium.explanation: The dashboard table is declared as 'var table t = table.new(...)'. The 'var' keyword in Pine Script means 'create this object once and never recreate it'. After the indicator loads, the table is pinned at the original position. Changing the Position input (Top Right, Bottom Left, etc.) in settings has no effect until you remove and re-add the indicator. Users will try to move the dashboard and get confused when nothing happens.
medium.location: Dashboard section — the `var table t = table.new(...)` declaration
medium.fixLocation: The `var table t` declaration and every call to f_row() inside the dashboard block

medium.title: Dashboard shows '0 / N confirmations' when trend is flat — looks like a broken display
medium.explanation: When neither biasUp nor biasDn is true (the EMAs are tangled or going sideways), confShown is hard-coded to zero. The dashboard then shows '0 / 4' or similar in the Confirmations row, which looks like an error rather than 'no trend right now'. Showing the higher of the long or short counts gives the user useful information about which direction is closer to triggering even during a sideways period.
medium.location: Signal engine section — the confShown assignment line
medium.fixLocation: The confShown assignment — just change the trailing `: 0` to `: math.max(nLong, nShort)`

medium.title: Session level detection silently fails on daily and weekly chart timeframes
medium.explanation: The session tracker uses time(timeframe.period, sessSpec, sessTz) where the default session is '0930-1600'. On a daily or weekly chart, each bar's timestamp is at midnight (start of day or week), which does not fall inside a 09:30-16:00 window. The result: sessNow is always false, curSessHi/Lo never accumulate, and pSessHi/Lo stay na forever. The Previous Session High/Low lines simply never appear on daily/weekly charts with no error message to explain why.
medium.location: Session accumulation — the `sessNow = not na(time(...))` assignment
medium.fixLocation: The sessNow assignment line — prepend the _intraday guard

low.title: The very first supply/demand zone confirmed can never earn the 'sweep' grade
low.explanation: The sweep grade point (awarded when the pivot ran above a prior pivot's high then closed back below it) requires priorPH / priorPL to already hold a value. Both start as na. So the first supply zone and first demand zone ever detected will always have swept=false, losing one possible grade point. On a short chart history this could mean the only visible zones are undergraded. In practice on a long chart history, only the very first historical zones are affected.
low.location: Supply/demand zone creation blocks — `swept = useSweep and not na(priorPH) and ...`
low.fixLocation: Documentation / awareness only — no change required

low.title: Zone freshness dimming is binary — a zone tapped 10 times looks the same as one tapped once
low.explanation: The dimTapped feature adds a fixed 14 transparency units to any zone whose tap count is above 0. Whether a zone has been tested once or ten times, it looks identical after the first tap. In real supply-and-demand analysis, a heavily-retested zone is much weaker than a freshly-formed one. A progressive dimming (each tap adds a bit more transparency) would communicate zone strength more accurately.
low.location: barstate.islast re-grade styling loops — the `tr` transparency calculation
low.fixLocation: Both the supply and demand re-grade loops inside the `if showZones and barstate.islast` block

low.title: If all 4 confirmation toggles are disabled and reqConf stays at 2, every crossover signals
low.explanation: effReq = math.min(reqConf, nReq) where nReq counts enabled confirmations. If useHTF, useMom, useVol, and useLoc are all turned OFF, nReq = 0, so effReq = math.min(2, 0) = 0. The condition nLong >= 0 is always true. Every single EMA-reclaim crossover in the correct bias direction fires a signal with no filtering at all. There is no warning in the dashboard or on the chart that this degenerate state exists.
low.location: Signal engine — effReq calculation and the signal gating logic
low.fixLocation: Beginning of the `if showDash and barstate.islast` block

improvements.title: OPTIONAL: Show confirmation count on the signal arrow (e.g. 'BUY 3/4')
improvements.description: Change the text parameter in plotshape() from 'BUY'/'SELL' to include the count: str.tostring(nLong) + '/' + str.tostring(nReq) appended to the label. This lets you see signal quality at a glance without opening the dashboard, and makes it immediately obvious whether a signal is barely passing (2/4) or very strong (4/4).

improvements.title: OPTIONAL: Use alert() instead of alertcondition() for richer message content
improvements.description: alertcondition() fires a static string. Replacing it with alert() calls inside the 'if buy' and 'if sell' blocks lets you embed dynamic info: ticker, timeframe, confirmation count, whether the signal was near a demand/supply zone, and zone grade. Example: alert('BUY ' + syminfo.ticker + ' | ' + str.tostring(nLong) + '/' + str.tostring(nReq) + ' confirms', alert.freq_once_per_bar_close)

improvements.title: OPTIONAL: Add active zone count to the dashboard
improvements.description: Add a row showing 'Supply zones: N | Demand: M' using array.size(supBoxes) and array.size(demBoxes). This lets traders instantly see whether the auto-adapt is filtering aggressively (showing only 1-2 zones) or permissively (6+ zones visible).

improvements.title: OPTIONAL: Add a 'no confirmations enabled' visual warning to the dashboard
improvements.description: When nReq == 0 (all confirmation toggles off), the dashboard Confirmations row shows '0 / 0' but gives no indication that every crossover will now signal. A red 'All filters disabled' message in the verdict row would prevent this surprise.

improvements.title: OPTIONAL: Show EMA separation strength in the dashboard bias row
improvements.description: The 'This TF bias' row shows only Up/Down/Flat. Adding the EMA spread as a percentage of price — (emaFast - emaSlow) / close * 100 — gives traders a sense of trend conviction: 0.05% spread = weak/choppy, 0.5%+ = strong trend. A thin bar or just a number next to the arrow would work.

files.type: Indicator (overlay) — pure alert engine, draws nothing by default
files.description: Fires TradingView alerts the instant chart price enters a supply or demand zone defined on a higher timeframe. Supports up to 3 independent timeframe slots (e.g. 15m, 30m, 1h simultaneously). Features: intrabar (wick) or on-close trigger mode, re-arm-on-exit anti-spam latch (one ping per fresh tap), cooldown, per-session alert cap, strict re-arm option, JSON/webhook payload format, and an optional debug visualization layer.

badges.label: Non-Repainting
badges.note: HTF zones come from confirmed ta.pivothigh/ta.pivotlow with lookahead=barmerge.lookahead_off. ta.valuewhen propagates confirmed pivot values forward. Zones never reference future bars.

badges.label: Alerts Match Logic
badges.note: alert() is called in the same if-blocks that update armed/pending/lastBar state. The fire condition is internally consistent throughout all 6 channels.

badges.label: HTF Lookahead
badges.note: All three request.security calls use lookahead=barmerge.lookahead_off. ta.pivothigh(len,len) inside f_htfZone confirms len bars after the pivot — the proven non-repainting HTF idiom.

badges.label: Anti-Spam Logic
badges.note: Re-arm latch, cooldown deferral (not drop), session cap, and strict re-arm all implemented correctly. One first-bar edge case (see HIGH issue).

high.title: First-bar false alert — a spurious alert can fire on load if price sits inside a watched zone
high.explanation: Every channel's entry-edge detection looks like: s1SupEnter = s1SupInRaw and not s1SupInRaw[1]. On bar 0 (the very first bar in history), s1SupInRaw[1] references the bar before history starts — Pine returns na for it. In Pine Script, 'not na' evaluates to true (na is treated as false, so its negation is true). Result: on bar 0, if price is inside a zone, the code sees inRaw=true AND not-was-inside=true, fires the entry edge, sets pending=true, and an alert fires. On a live chart this means: every time you add the indicator or TradingView recompiles it, if price currently sits inside a watched zone, a ghost alert fires immediately.
high.location: Entry detection section — all 6 sNXxxEnter assignment lines
high.fixLocation: The 6 sNXxxEnter assignment lines — find them all in the 'first-entry edge' comment block

medium.title: Per-session alert cap resets at UTC midnight, not the trader's actual session boundary
medium.explanation: newSession = timeframe.change('D') detects a new UTC calendar day (midnight UTC). For US traders on Eastern time, this means the cap resets at 7pm ET — before the overnight Globex session, not at the 9:30am open. On a volatile overnight session, the cap could be exhausted entirely before the regular session starts. The impact is zero when spamMaxPerSession = 0 (the default, unlimited), but any non-zero cap will behave unexpectedly for non-UTC traders.
medium.location: The newSession variable definition and the `if newSession` session-reset block
medium.fixLocation: The newSession line — replace or augment the timeframe.change('D') call

medium.title: Deferred (cooldown-blocked) alert message reports the current zone bounds, not the zone that was actually tapped
medium.explanation: When a zone entry is blocked by the cooldown, the pending flag is set immediately but the alert fires later (on the first bar after the cooldown expires). The alert message is built at fire time using the CURRENT values of s1SupBot / s1SupTop. If a new HTF pivot confirmed during the cooldown window, those values now point to a DIFFERENT zone than the one that was originally tapped. The message will show the wrong price band — potentially misleading the trader about which level to watch.
medium.location: Latch pending taps block (where pend is set to true) and the alert emit block
medium.fixLocation: var state block (add new vars), each pend-set if-block (store bounds), each emit if-block (use stored bounds)

medium.title: Only the most recently confirmed pivot is watched per slot+side — double tops and zone clusters are missed
medium.explanation: Inside f_htfZone, ta.valuewhen(not na(ph), high[len], 0) uses occurrence=0 which means 'the most recent time the pivot fired'. Only one zone band per slot+side is ever active. If there is a double top on the 30m chart (two supply pivots at slightly different levels), only the newer one is watched — the older level is silently ignored. In SMC analysis, zone clusters and confluent levels are often the most important ones.
medium.location: f_htfZone function — the ta.valuewhen calls with occurrence=0
medium.fix:
// The cleanest lightweight fix is to watch occurrence=0 AND occurrence=1
// (most recent and second-most-recent pivot) and OR the entry tests.
// This requires modifying f_htfZone to return 8 values instead of 4:

f_htfZone(int len, bool wickBody) =>
    ph = ta.pivothigh(len, len)
    pl = ta.pivotlow(len, len)
    supTop0 = ta.valuewhen(not na(ph), high[len], 0)
    supBot0 = ta.valuewhen(not na(ph), wickBody ? math.min(open[len], close[len]) : low[len], 0)
    supTop1 = ta.valuewhen(not na(ph), high[len], 1)   // second-most-recent
    supBot1 = ta.valuewhen(not na(ph), wickBody ? math.min(open[len], close[len]) : low[len], 1)
    demBot0 = ta.valuewhen(not na(pl), low[len], 0)
    demTop0 = ta.valuewhen(not na(pl), wickBody ? math.max(open[len], close[len]) : high[len], 0)
    demBot1 = ta.valuewhen(not na(pl), low[len], 1)
    demTop1 = ta.valuewhen(not na(pl), wickBody ? math.max(open[len], close[len]) : high[len], 1)
    [supTop0, supBot0, supTop1, supBot1, demTop0, demBot0, demTop1, demBot1]
// Then unpack all 8 values from each request.security call and
// combine inRaw tests with OR: inRaw = test_zone0 or test_zone1
medium.fixLocation: f_htfZone function body plus all 3 request.security call sites and their downstream inRaw tests

low.title: Slot 3 custom pivot legs defaults to 15 but mode defaults to Inherit — the 15 is never used by default
low.explanation: s3LenMode defaults to 'Inherit', so s3LenEff = zLenDefault = 10 regardless of s3Len. The s3Len input shows 15 as its default, which looks like Slot 3 is configured for a longer lookback — but it is not. The 15 only activates if the user explicitly switches s3LenMode to Custom. Any user inspecting settings will see 15 and think there is a difference from the other slots, when there isn't.
low.location: TF Slot 3 input group — the s3Len input.int() call
low.fixLocation: The s3Len input.int() line in the TF Slot 3 group

low.title: spamMaxPerSession is per-channel (6 separate counters), not per-indicator total
low.explanation: With spamMaxPerSession=5 and all 3 slots + both sides active, you can receive up to 30 alerts per UTC day (5 per channel × 6 channels). The tooltip says 'per slot+side' which is technically accurate but easy to misread. A trader who sets the cap to 5 expecting at most 5 alerts per day will actually get up to 30 on an active session. The behaviour is correct — just not intuitive from the settings panel.
low.fixLocation: The spamMaxPerSession input.int tooltip string — update text only

improvements.title: OPTIONAL: Watch two pivot zones per slot+side (second-most-recent) for double-top/bottom clusters
improvements.description: As described in Medium issue #3, adding occurrence=1 to each ta.valuewhen call and OR-ing the inRaw tests would catch confluent zone clusters with minimal code overhead. This is the single highest-value improvement for SMC traders who rely on zone clusters as stronger signals.

improvements.title: OPTIONAL: Lightweight armed/disarmed indicator without full debug mode
improvements.description: Debug mode shows full-width zone boxes — useful during setup but visually distracting in live trading. A lighter alternative: plot a small colored triangle or dot at the bar where each channel fires, giving a subtle visual log of alert history without permanent zone rectangles on the chart.

improvements.title: OPTIONAL: Include zone age (HTF bars since pivot confirmed) in the alert message
improvements.description: Fresh pivots (confirmed very recently) are generally more reliable than stale ones from weeks ago. Capturing the bar_index at pivot-confirmation time (via a second ta.valuewhen call) and computing the elapsed HTF bars lets you add a freshness note to the message: e.g. '15m SUPPLY tap | zone age: 12 bars'.

improvements.title: OPTIONAL: Mini status table showing current zone bounds and armed state
improvements.description: Currently the indicator draws nothing by default (pure alert engine). A small, toggleable table in the corner showing each enabled slot's current supply/demand price bands and whether each channel is armed or waiting to re-arm would let traders verify the engine is tracking the right levels without enabling full debug mode.

improvements.title: OPTIONAL: Support 4 or 5 timeframe slots
improvements.description: The 3-slot architecture is hardcoded. Pine v6 does not allow looping over request.security calls, so additional slots require copy-pasting the unrolled block pattern. Adding slots 4 and 5 as optional disabled groups (same pattern as slots 2 and 3) would be a straightforward extension.
